carbon_policy_signal.item: Ireland tackles EU ETS reform amid competing country demands; outcome on cap stringency and compliance cost over summer 2026.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: EUA trajectory depends on whether member states weaken or defend the cap. Outcome flags fossil-generation marginal cost, industrial fuel-switch economics, and power-curve shape into 2027.

freshness_caveat: Coal data stale (187 days old, as of 2025-12-26); Clean Dark stale (182 days old, as of 2025-12-31). Fuel-switch regime and dark-spread risk unconfirmed; rely on current Clean Spark (93rd-pctile) and GB/DE Power spreads for regime assessment.
risk_flags: Storage 14.7pp below 5-yr seasonal average; refill pace Q3–Q4 will set H2 power curve risk.
GB Power at 137.29 EUR/MWh (95th-pctile) vs renewables at 30.2% of load (24th-pctile); thermal call extended into summer.
Iran rejects Hormuz security plan; escalation could raise LNG shipping costs and push TTF supply-risk premium into Q3.
Coal data stale (187 days old, 7th-pctile at 96 USD/t); Clean Dark stale (182 days old, 49th-pctile at 27.95 EUR/MWh); fuel-switch regime unconfirmed.

base.summary: Renewables recover modestly; storage injection continues at seasonal pace; GB power moderates into 80–90th-pctile range.

downside.summary: French heat wave subsides, renewables recover, GB surplus rebuilds; storage injection accelerates; thermal call unwinds.

upside.summary: Hormuz escalation or LNG shipping disruption; Iran tensions spike shipping costs, TTF supply-risk premium reasserts sharply.

themes: GB power spike, renewable drought, storage deficit
Geopolitical tension on Hormuz, Russian fuel supply disruption
ETS reform negotiations under Irish presidency
French heat wave, grid strain, industrial policy uncertainty
top_drivers: GB Power spike (95th-pctile) driven by renewable collapse (24th-pctile) and storage deficit (14.7pp below seasonal); thermal call extended into summer.
Geopolitical tail risk: Iran rejects Hormuz security plan, Ukrainian drone strikes degrade Russian fuel production; both raise LNG shipping uncertainty and gas supply-risk premium.
ETS reform under Irish presidency creates medium-term carbon policy uncertainty; competing member demands on cap stringency.
top_takeaway: GB Power at 95th-percentile amid tight renewables (24th-pctile) and storage 14.7pp below seasonal; geopolitical friction (Iran rejects Hormuz plan, Ukraine strikes Russian refineries) adds tail risk to LNG/gas curve.

watchlist.why: GB at 95th-pctile (137.29 EUR/MWh) while DE at 76th-pctile (140.28 EUR/MWh); supply/interconnector asymmetry may widen or compress on renewable recovery.
48.86% full, 14.7pp below seasonal; injection pace over July–August determines H2 gas/power curve floor and forced-thermal exposure.
Iran rejection of security plan raises geopolitical friction; monitor shipping delays, route-cost spikes, and TTF supply-risk premium (currently 54th-pctile at 43.44 EUR/MWh).
No-confidence vote risk; potential for nuclear outages or demand-side policy; upward pressure on French DA/Cal+1 spreads vs. system.
Competing member-state demands on cap stringency; outcome over summer 2026 sets EUA price ceiling (currently 33.16 EUR/tCO2, 38th-pctile, flat YTD).

watchlist_dated.name: Irish EU presidency ETS negotiations begin
watchlist_dated.why: Sets tone for H2 2026 carbon policy; outcome flags whether EUA stringency increases or weakens.
